rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Jennifer Kwoks Notice Of Motion And Motion To Temporarily Suspend The Trustee Of The Stan Kwong Trust On In The Alternative Limit Trustees Power
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION, THE HEARING IS CONTINUED FROM 9:00 AM TO 11:00 AM ON FRIDAY MARCH 19, 2021. The court grants Respondent's unopposed Request for Judicial Notice. The court grants Petitioner's unopposed Request for Judicial Notice. Notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) Petitioner Jennifer Kwok's Motion to Temporarily Suspend Respondent/Trustee Jeanne Kwong, or in the alternative, Limit Trustee's Powers is DENIED. There is no showing that a motion is the correct procedural vehicle with which to seek this relief. Probate Code section 15642 allows the court to remove a trustee "on its own motion" or "on petition of a settlor, cotrustee, or beneficiary under Section 17200," or order a trustee to surrender trust property "on [the court's] own motion or on petition of a cotrustee or beneficiary" (sub. (e)). A petition is the correct vehicle with which to seek the requested relief, and Petitioner has had one pending for over a year, since December 2019. It has been before the court on regular hearing twice, in October 2020 and January 2021, and will be again on April 5, 2021. Petitioner's allegations in support of her requests to remove or suspend the Trustee for failure to distribute the remaining Trust assets have not changed materially since the Petition was filed in 2019. The court is aware of the pending Petition's allegations, and declined to enter a ruling on the Petition at both the October 2020 and January 2021 hearings. The court also denied Petitioner's January 5, 2021 Ex Parte Application to Suspend Trustee, which is almost identical to the text of this motion's memorandum of points and authorities. Insofar as Petitioner invites the court to consider this motion as a motion for reconsideration under CCP section 1008, the motion is denied as procedurally defective (see CCP section 1008(b)). This motion is denied without comment on the relative or potential merits of the pending Verified Petition for Order Suspending, or in the Alternative Removing Trustee, or on the terms of the 2017 Stipulation between the parties. Respondent shall prepare and lodge with the court clerk in Department 204 a hard-copy proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on March 18, 2021. ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION, THE HEARING IS CONTINUED FROM 9:00 AM TO 11:00 AM ON FRIDAY MARCH 19, 2021. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925#, participant ID 0#, and passcode 722809#.
